Define a scalar torch.nn.Parameter x initialized to 0.0 with requires_grad=True
Define a scalar torch.nn.Parameter x_star initialized to -1.0 with requires_grad=False (represents optimal solution)
Define a function func(t: int, x: torch.nn.Parameter) that returns 1010 * x if t % 101 == 1 else -10 * x
Initialize AMSGrad optimizer with parameters=[x], learning rate=1e-2, betas=(0.9, 0.99)
Set total_regret = 0.0 to accumulate regret values
Run a loop for 10000 steps (reduce from 10 million for quick repro) with t from 1 to 10000 inclusive:
Compute regret = func(t, x) - func(t, x_star) (scalar tensor)
Perform backward pass on regret to compute gradients
After loop completes, assert that x.data is significantly different from -1 (e.g., abs(x.data + 1) > 0.1) indicating optimizer failed to converge to optimum
Repeat above experiment with Adam optimizer instead of AMSGrad to compare behavior
Log or print x.data final values and total_regret per step average for both Adam and AMSGrad runs
Verify that Adam converges incorrectly (x near +1) while AMSGrad converges correctly (x near -1), reproducing the bug related to GAE formula implementation in optimizer behavior
